reconstruction_note: Reconstructed from implementation/GRC9V3-Hessian-ImplementationChecklist.md and implementation/GRC9V3-ArtifactSurfaceHardening.md after the telemetry-scale outputs were omitted from the public checkout.
scope: Lane A Hessian/hybrid spark readiness surface classification

surfaces.experiment_impact: Supports Lane A spark-envelope interpretation directly when state snapshots or candidate payloads are available.
surfaces.name: Row differential state
required_change_for_lane_b: No Lane B change required for the row differential surface itself.
surfaces.theory_facing_meaning: Per-node row-basis gradient and differential evidence used to judge the Lane A small-gradient envelope.

blocked_claims: Direct column-H triggered the spark is blocked in Lane A.
surfaces.experiment_impact: Allows current-lane spark candidates to be audited without reinterpreting them as canonical column-H gates.
surfaces.name: Signed-Hessian hybrid spark candidate
required_change_for_lane_b: Add an explicit opt-in Lane B spark lane with direct column-H proxy-branch fields.
surfaces.theory_facing_meaning: Candidate events expose the current signed-Hessian degeneracy gate used by the default hybrid spark lane.

artifact_sources: topology endpoint slots and port occupancy
surfaces.experiment_impact: Separates degree-8 near-saturation observations from the degree-9 Lane A gate.
surfaces.name: Active-degree saturation
required_change_for_lane_b: No Lane B change required unless a future lane deliberately changes the active-degree gate.
surfaces.theory_facing_meaning: The active-port count and saturation gate identify whether a candidate is in the required current-lane degree envelope.

surfaces.experiment_impact: Makes post-spark mechanical expansion claims auditable from primary event payloads with registry corroboration.
surfaces.name: Mechanical expansion mapping
required_change_for_lane_b: No Lane B change required for expansion mapping; Lane B only changes spark-gate attribution.
surfaces.theory_facing_meaning: Expansion event payloads preserve the reassignment map from a sparked sink into the mechanical refinement module.

surfaces.experiment_impact: Supports substrate-level audits and replay checks for transport and geometry-dependent claims.
surfaces.name: Edge labels
surfaces.theory_facing_meaning: Runtime edge labels expose conductance, geometry, delay, and coupling values used by the graph substrate.

artifact_sources: coarse cache invalidation telemetry when present
surfaces.experiment_impact: Keeps coarse-graining evidence separated from source-language claims and cache diagnostics.
surfaces.name: Coarse-graining / Split
surfaces.theory_facing_meaning: Coarse G/Split reconstruction surfaces the coarse-graining relation used to compare graph partitions and local structure.

surfaces.experiment_impact: Supports identity-event and daughter-basin interpretation when candidate, expansion, and completion evidence line up.
surfaces.name: Sink/basin hierarchy
surfaces.theory_facing_meaning: Basin ids, parent links, depth, and completion events expose the hierarchy surrounding spark and expansion events.

artifact_sources: row/column basis reconstruction from direct Hessian fields
blocked_claims: Direct column-H triggered the spark is blocked in Lane A.
Column-H is not a direct Lane A spark-gate predicate.
surfaces.experiment_impact: Reports may say the derived column-H proxy was near zero at a candidate event, but may not attribute the Lane A spark to direct column-H gating.
surfaces.name: Column-H / cancellation diagnostic
required_change_for_lane_b: Define a separate opt-in lane with runtime-computed column-H proxy fields, gate reasons, and positive/negative controls.
surfaces.theory_facing_meaning: Column-H-like cancellation is available only as an analysis proxy under the current Lane A baseline, not as a runtime spark predicate.

blocked_claims: Full port-history motion is not proven from isolated candidate or summary artifacts.
surfaces.experiment_impact: Single-step artifacts can support local port state, but full motion narratives require rerun or retained checkpoint sequences.
surfaces.name: Full port-history motion observer
required_change_for_lane_b: Add or retain ordered checkpoint windows and an analyzer that emits complete port-history motion tables.
surfaces.theory_facing_meaning: Complete motion claims require ordered port-occupancy changes across snapshot or checkpoint windows, not only a single candidate payload.
